job_title: Internal Audit - New York - Associate, Data Analytics - 3765660
strengths: Experience building ETL pipelines and data models

weaknesses: Limited explicit ML algorithm implementation (SVM/Random Forest/Boosting)

improvement_bullets: Implemented supervised machine learning techniques (Random Forest, Logistic Regression) within Python to classify high-risk loan segments and automate default probability reporting, enhancing audit accuracy.
Engineered and maintained automated CI/CD pipelines for data products using Git, ensuring high code quality and seamless deployment in accordance with SDLC best practices.
Utilized Python-based Natural Language Processing (NLP) to perform text analytics on unstructured operational datasets, successfully identifying patterns in service logs to optimize performance.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to translate complex business requirements into data-driven risk assessment models, directly supporting Compliance & Legal audit initiatives.
Developed robust clustering algorithms to segment customer populations for risk analysis, providing actionable insights for audit planning and continuous monitoring of portfolio performance.
cover_letter: Hiring Manager, Goldman Sachs. Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Internal Audit - New York - Associate, Data Analytics - 3765660 opportunity with Goldman Sachs. I am interested in this opportunity because of the firm’s reputation for operational excellence and its commitment to leveraging data-driven insights to mitigate complex financial risks. What interests me about working for Goldman Sachs is its proactive approach to internal audit and continuous monitoring, which sets a gold standard in the financial industry. I believe my proficiency in data modeling and my ability to automate audit-heavy workflows would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by focusing on technical accuracy and scalable automation. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in reporting and developed an automated ETL pipeline that reduced data processing time by 40%. The result was a significantly faster turnaround for executive decision-making, showcasing my ability to translate technical data solutions into tangible operational improvements that align directly with the core audit responsibilities of this role. Ultimately, I know that the Internal Audit - New York - Associate role at Goldman Sachs would allow me to bring my advanced SQL and Python skills to the creation of reusable tools and innovative audit solutions. Additionally, my experience in machine learning and data segmentation at my previous roles enabled me to develop my analytical skills in predictive modeling. In my recent Lending Risk Portfolio analysis, I applied regression techniques and supervised learning to identify high-risk borrower segments, which enabled more precise audit testing and data-driven risk management. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my expertise in statistical modeling and data visualization in working on risk audit and continuous monitoring initiatives at Goldman Sachs. I know that my technical background in Python and SQL and my commitment to engineering best practices align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss how my background in automating workflows and performing high-stakes risk analysis can support the audit team. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
